growth_records.cite: Park, Mee-Rye et al. Revealing oxidative pentose metabolism in new Pseudomonas putida isolates. Environmental Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.16296. [PMC10107873]
growth_records.snippet: P. taiwanensis VLB120 ... Glucose 0.55 +/- 0.07 (Growth rate mu_max, h-1).
growth_records.notes: Table 1, glucose column. mu_max=0.55 h-1 measured. td=ln2/0.55=1.26 h. No uptake/secretion rates in paper.

growth_records.cite: Park, Mee-Rye et al. Revealing oxidative pentose metabolism in new Pseudomonas putida isolates. Environmental Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.16296. [PMC10107873]
growth_records.snippet: P. taiwanensis VLB120 ... Xylose 0.20 +/- 0.01 (Growth rate mu_max, h-1).
growth_records.notes: Table 1, xylose column. mu_max=0.20 h-1 measured. td=ln2/0.20=3.47 h. VLB120 grows on xylose via native oxidative pathway. No uptake/secretion rates in paper.

growth_records.strain: VLB120 delta-C delta-3 pCom10lac_FspeKDO (L-lysine catabolism triple KO; heterologous Flavobacterium sp. lysine dioxygenase)

up.method: HPLC concentration time-course fitted to exponential-growth model (gPROMS), Table 1 rXyl (negative = uptake)
HILIC-HPLC time-course fit, Table 1 rLys (negative = uptake)

sec.method: HPLC (RI/UV) time-course fit, Table 1 rXlaXlt (positive = production, Weimberg intermediate overflow)

mfa.note: Phase I overflow of Weimberg intermediates

yields.overall_process: cHyl 8.7 ± 0.3 g/L (53.8 mM); Y_Hyl/X 1.68 ± 0.07 g/gCDW; Y_Hyl/Lys 0.89 ± 0.10 mol/mol; Y_Hyl/Xyl 0.38 ± 0.01 mol/mol; Y_Hyl/Xyl,net 0.48 ± 0.02 mol/mol; STY 98.6 ± 3.4 mg/L/h

growth_records.cite: Nerke et al. Engineering Pseudomonas taiwanensis VLB120 for regio- and stereospecific hydroxylation of L-lysine fueled by the Weimberg pathway. Microbial Cell Factories (2026). DOI:10.1186/s12934-026-02931-0. [PMC12930750]
growth_records.method: reported (extracellular rate estimated by fitting exponential growth model to biomass profile, 2 biological replicates); doubling time calculated td=ln2/0.125=5.545 h
growth_records.snippet: In the first phase, d-xylose is taken up, and exponential growth is visible with a growth rate of 0.125 h-1 ... mu 0.125 ± 0.003 ... r Xyl -4.88 ± 0.16 ... r XlaXlt 22.89 ± 0.22 ... r Hyl 0.366 ± 0.028 ... r Lys -0.43 ± 0.05
growth_records.notes: STR Phase I (+100 mM L-lysine). All specific rates from Table 1, estimated by fitting concentration profiles (D-xylose, L-lysine, hydroxy-L-lysine, xylonolactone/xylonate, biomass) to exponential-growth model (gPROMS, 2 replicates). Sign convention: negative Table-1 value = uptake, listed here as positive magnitude in uptake_rates. td=ln2/0.125=5.545 h. Overall metrics (Table 2) span both phases. OD450-to-CDW 0.2049 gCDW/L per OD [cited from ref 31]. M9 base salts only in Supplementary Table S1 (not in fulltext).

up.method: HPLC time-course fit, Table 1 rXlaXlt (negative in Phase II = uptake of accumulated intermediates)

mfa.note: Phase II, consumes previously secreted intermediates
Phase II (main production by cumulative amount, higher biomass)

yields.overall_process: cHyl 8.7 g/L; STY 98.6 mg/L/h; Y_Hyl/Xyl,net 0.48 mol/mol

growth_records.cite: Nerke et al. Engineering Pseudomonas taiwanensis VLB120 for regio- and stereospecific hydroxylation of L-lysine fueled by the Weimberg pathway. Microbial Cell Factories (2026). DOI:10.1186/s12934-026-02931-0. [PMC12930750]
growth_records.method: reported (exponential-model fit, Table 1); doubling time calculated td=ln2/0.007=99.0 h
growth_records.snippet: In the second growth phase, d-xylose is depleted, and the cells take up d-xylonolactone/d-xylonate for growth ... Y Hyl/X ... second growth phase (36.12 ± 4.31 mmol gCDW-1) ... r Hyl 0.244 ± 0.036 ... r Lys -0.21 ± 0.05 ... r XlaXlt -0.17 ±
growth_records.notes: STR Phase II (+100 mM L-lysine). Growth on Weimberg intermediates D-xylonolactone/D-xylonate (linear growth, mu=0.007/h). Rates from Table 1; negative Table values = uptake, given as positive magnitudes. td=ln2/0.007=99.0 h. Y_Hyl/X 36.12 mmol/gCDW in this phase drives overall biomass-specific product yield 1.68 g/gCDW.

up.method: HPLC time-course fit, Table 1 rXyl (Phase I, -L-lysine); higher than +L-lysine case (4.88)

sec.method: HPLC time-course fit, Table 1 rXlaXlt (Phase I production)

mfa.note: Phase I, no L-lysine; faster than with L-lysine

yields.note: no product (no L-lysine); Phase II mu 0.007 ± 0.000/h, rXlaXlt -0.13 ± 0.02 mmol/gCDW/h (intermediate re-uptake)

growth_records.cite: Nerke et al. Engineering Pseudomonas taiwanensis VLB120 for regio- and stereospecific hydroxylation of L-lysine fueled by the Weimberg pathway. Microbial Cell Factories (2026). DOI:10.1186/s12934-026-02931-0. [PMC12930750]
growth_records.method: reported (exponential-model fit, Table 1); doubling time calculated td=ln2/0.155=4.472 h
growth_records.snippet: In comparison to the cultivations without l-lysine, the biotransformations showed a reduced growth rate and a reduced d-xylose uptake rate ... -L-lysine Phase I mu 0.155 ± 0.002 ... r Xyl -6.06 ± 0.24 ... r XlaXlt 21.89 ± 0.57
growth_records.notes: STR reference cultivation WITHOUT L-lysine (Table 1, -L-lysine columns). Phase I mu=0.155/h (faster than +L-lysine 0.125) and higher xylose uptake 6.06 vs 4.88 mmol/gCDW/h, showing biotransformation burden slows growth/uptake. td=ln2/0.155=4.472 h. Phase II (-L-lysine): mu 0.007/h, rXlaXlt -0.13 mmol/gCDW/h (near-zero growth, no product; folded into note).

growth_records.strain: VLB120 GRC3 Δ8Δ pykA -tap attTn7::P14g AtPAL-aroGfbr-pheA(T310) (engineered t-cinnamate producer; heterologous Arabidopsis PAL)
growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM, Hartmans et al. 1989, 2x mineral salts stock) with 20 mM glucose
growth_records.cite: Otto, Maike et al. Evolution of aromatic amino acid biosynthesis and application to the fine-tuned phylogenetic positioning of enteric bacteria. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00312. [PMC6882275]
growth_records.method: not quantified (growth-associated; strains showed lowered growth rate on heterologous overexpression)
growth_records.snippet: When cultivated on glucose, the strain accumulated 3.80 +/- 0.20 mM t-cinnamate, corresponding to a yield of 25.9 +/- 0.1% Cmol Cmol-1, with a volumetric productivity of 0.10 +/- 0.00 mM h-1 (Figure 4). [Table 3: Shake flask glucose, tap st
growth_records.notes: Engineered P. taiwanensis VLB120 (genome-reduced GRC3) expressing Arabidopsis PAL for t-cinnamate. Table 3, shake-flask glucose, strain '-tap'. Product = heterologous t-cinnamate; volumetric productivity 0.10 mM/h, yield 25.9% Cmol/Cmol, titer 3.80 mM. Growth rate not numerically reported. Glucose 20 mM initial, fully consumed.

growth_records.strain: VLB120 GRC3 Δ8Δ pykA -tap attTn7::P14g AtPAL-aroGfbr-pheA(T310)
growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM, Hartmans et al. 1989, 2x mineral salts) with 40 mM glycerol
growth_records.cite: Otto, Maike et al. Evolution of aromatic amino acid biosynthesis and application to the fine-tuned phylogenetic positioning of enteric bacteria. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00312. [PMC6882275]
growth_records.method: not quantified (reduced growth rate on glycerol vs glucose)
growth_records.snippet: From glycerol, the t-cinnamate titer was further increased to 6.33 +/- 0.12 mM, corresponding to a yield of 47.5 +/- 0.9% Cmol Cmol-1... volumetric productivity on glycerol is slightly lower compared to glucose (0.09 +/- 0.01 mM h-1). [Tabl
growth_records.notes: Table 3, shake-flask glycerol, strain '-tap'. t-cinnamate 6.33 mM, yield 47.5% Cmol/Cmol, productivity 0.09 mM/h. Higher yield but lower productivity than glucose owing to reduced growth rate on glycerol. Glycerol 40 mM initial.

growth_records.strain: VLB120 GRC3 Δ8Δ pykA -tap attTn7::P14g AtPAL-aroGfbr-pheA(T310) attTn7::P14f AtPAL (two PAL copies)
growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM) with 20 mM glucose
growth_records.cite: Otto, Maike et al. Evolution of aromatic amino acid biosynthesis and application to the fine-tuned phylogenetic positioning of enteric bacteria. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00312. [PMC6882275]
growth_records.snippet: ...with 3.34 +/- 0.07 mM produced from glucose... yields of 22.8 +/- 0.5... Cmol Cmol-1. [Table 3: Shake flask glucose, second AtPAL-copy strain; Productivity 0.13 +/- 0.00 mM h-1]
growth_records.notes: Table 3, shake-flask glucose, two-AtPAL-copy strain. t-cinnamate 3.34 mM, yield 22.8% Cmol/Cmol, productivity 0.13 mM/h. Slightly lower titer/yield than single-copy but higher productivity.

growth_records.strain: VLB120 GRC3 Δ8Δ pykA -tap attTn7::P14g AtPAL-aroGfbr-pheA(T310) attTn7::P14f AtPAL (two PAL copies)
growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM) with 40 mM glycerol
growth_records.cite: Otto, Maike et al. Evolution of aromatic amino acid biosynthesis and application to the fine-tuned phylogenetic positioning of enteric bacteria. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00312. [PMC6882275]
growth_records.snippet: ...5.25 +/- 0.22 mM from glycerol, which corresponds to yields of ... 38.9 +/- 1.6% Cmol Cmol-1. [Table 3: Shake flask glycerol, second AtPAL-copy strain; Productivity 0.11 +/- 0.00 mM h-1]
growth_records.notes: Table 3, shake-flask glycerol, two-AtPAL-copy strain. t-cinnamate 5.25 mM, yield 38.9% Cmol/Cmol, productivity 0.11 mM/h.

growth_records.strain: VLB120 GRC3 Δ8Δ pykA -tap attTn7::P14g AtPAL-aroGfbr-pheA(T310)
growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM) with glucose (fed-batch: 5 mM glucose feed shots)
growth_records.cite: Otto, Maike et al. Evolution of aromatic amino acid biosynthesis and application to the fine-tuned phylogenetic positioning of enteric bacteria. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00312. [PMC6882275]
growth_records.snippet: ...GRC3 Δ8Δ pykA -tap attTn7::P14g AtPAL-aroGfbr-pheA T310 accumulated 17.2 +/- 0.28 mM of t-cinnamate from glucose, corresponding to a yield of 11.2 +/- 0.6% (Cmol Cmol-1) and a volumetric productivity of 0.19 +/- 0.20 mM h-1... culture re
growth_records.notes: Table 3, fed-batch glucose, single-copy '-tap' strain. t-cinnamate 17.2 mM, yield 11.2% Cmol/Cmol, overall productivity 0.19 mM/h. Fed-batch in DASbox, dO2-triggered glucose feed. Final OD600 ~12.7 at ~65 h. Lower yield than shake-flask due to biomass diversion.

growth_records.strain: VLB120 GRC3 Δ8Δ pykA -tap attTn7::P14g AtPAL-aroGfbr-pheA(T310) attTn7::P14f AtPAL (two PAL copies)
growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM) with glucose (fed-batch, 5 mM glucose feed shots)

sec.units: mM/h (overall volumetric productivity; 0.55 +/- 0.08 within first 25 h, 0.16 +/- 0.04 after 38 h)

growth_records.cite: Otto, Maike et al. Evolution of aromatic amino acid biosynthesis and application to the fine-tuned phylogenetic positioning of enteric bacteria. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00312. [PMC6882275]
growth_records.snippet: ...P14f AtPAL produced up to 33.2 +/- 2.4 mM t-cinnamate from glucose... the productivity throughout the whole cultivation on glucose was 0.30 +/- 0.02 mM h-1... within the first 25 h is 0.55 +/- 0.08 mM h-1, which is reduced after 38 h to
growth_records.notes: Table 3, fed-batch glucose, two-AtPAL-copy strain. t-cinnamate 33.2 mM, yield 21.4% Cmol/Cmol, overall productivity 0.30 mM/h (early 0.55, late 0.16 - product inhibition of PAL). Highest glucose fed-batch titer.

growth_records.strain: VLB120 GRC3 Δ8Δ pykA -tap attTn7::P14g AtPAL-aroGfbr-pheA(T310) attTn7::P14f AtPAL (two PAL copies)
growth_records.medium: Mineral salts medium (MSM) with glycerol (fed-batch, 10 mM glycerol feed shots)
growth_records.cite: Otto, Maike et al. Evolution of aromatic amino acid biosynthesis and application to the fine-tuned phylogenetic positioning of enteric bacteria. Frontiers in Bioengineering and Biotechnology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fbioe.2019.00312. [PMC6882275]
growth_records.snippet: ...33.5 +/- 2.7 mM from glycerol in a fed-batch fermentation... Fed-batch glycerol Final titer 33.5 +/- 2.7 mM; Yield 36.1 +/- 0.08% Cmol Cmol-1; Productivity 0.15 +/- 0.00 mM h-1. [Table 3]
growth_records.notes: Table 3, fed-batch glycerol, two-AtPAL-copy strain. t-cinnamate 33.5 mM (highest titer in study), yield 36.1% Cmol/Cmol, productivity 0.15 mM/h. The single-copy '-tap' strain on glycerol fed-batch (n=1) gave 19.8 mM, 47.8% Cmol, 0.13 mM/h.
